critique.summary: Reviewed README.md, schema.py, and serializer.py. The package violates its stated contract in several concrete ways: it silently injects defaults for required inputs, emits the wrong type for count, and its validator both skips all but the first record and ignores schema types.

findings.description: serializer.py:to_record silently supplies version=1 when data lacks "version" via data.get("version", 1). README.md and schema.py both state that version is required and has no default, so a missing version must be rejected rather than defaulted.

serializer.py:to_record silently supplies items=[] when data lacks "items" via data.get("items", []). items is marked required in schema.FIELDS, so this injects a missing required field instead of reporting an error.

serializer.py:to_record stores "count" as str(n) even though README.md says count is an INTEGER equal to len(items) and schema.py declares type "int". For example, items=[1,2] produces count="2" instead of 2.

serializer.py:validate only inspects records[0]. It returns the required-field check for the first record and ignores every later record, contradicting the documented contract that validate(records) validates every record.

serializer.py:validate does not validate schema types at all; it only checks that required field names are present in the first record. As written, it accepts records whose values violate schema.FIELDS, such as count being a string and items being a non-list, even though its docstring says records must satisfy the schema.
